behavior_summary: Handles player interaction with a gravestone item: a short 'U8P_GRAVE_NS::look' description is shown when examined, and a more detailed epitaph reader runs when the item is used.
Both U8P_GRAVE_NS::look and use perform local bookkeeping on the item (set info / process-exclude) before presenting text to the player.
The detailed reader (U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB) obtains the item's quality value to select one of many hard-coded epitaph strings and calls Grave::read to display the chosen epitaph.
Use launches the reader asynchronously via spawn so the use event returns immediately while the epitaph is presented by a spawned process.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRAVE_NS::look: mark item (set info / process-exclude) then call Item::bark to show the short description and return.
U8P_GRAVE_NS::use: perform same item bookkeeping, then spawn U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB to run the detailed reader asynchronously.

U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB: call Item::getQuality, store result in a local ([BP-02h]), set a small flag/byte ([BP-04h] = 0x1B), use the quality value as a switch to choose an epitaph string, then call Grave::read with that string and the flag.
open_questions: Exact meaning of the info key set during 'set info' / process-exclude (what 0x210 or equivalent encodes) — inferred only from opcodes.
Precise semantics of the constant 0x1B stored at [BP-04h] and how Grave::read uses it (formatting/line-count/flag hypothesis).
Full mapping between Item::getQuality return values and epitaph meanings beyond the excerpt (the mapping exists but its design intent is inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an examine/U8P_GRAVE_NS::look action on a gravestone by marking the item (info/process-exclude) and producing a short descriptive bark ('gravestone ') to the viewing actor.
entry_conditions: Event 0: player/actor performs a 'U8P_GRAVE_NS::look' (examine) action on the gravestone item (triggered by entering the U8P_GRAVE_NS::look/observe area).

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::look
function_map.role: Short-examine handler: set item bookkeeping flags then call Item::bark to display a one-line description.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' event: set item bookkeeping flags and spawn the detailed reader (U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB) to present the epitaph asynchronously.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB
function_map.role: Detailed epitaph reader: fetch item quality via Item::getQuality, select a hard-coded epitaph string via a large switch, and call Grave::read to present it.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine call used to display a short item message/bark to the actor (used by U8P_GRAVE_NS::look()).

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Engine call returning a numeric quality/subtype used as the selector for epitaph variants.

function_map.name: Grave::read
function_map.role: Engine/UI routine that presents multi-line epitaph text to the player; receives a small flag/word and the text pointer plus context.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Usecode opcode to create a new process for asynchronous execution (used to run U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB from use()).

internal_calls.purpose: Launch the detailed epitaph reader asynchronously so the 'use' event returns quickly.

external_calls.purpose: Show the short one-line description 'gravestone ' to the viewing actor; parameters: item pointer and literal string.
Spawn a process to run U8P_GRAVE_NS::00CB with the item/pid context as argument.
Retrieve the item's quality/subtype value used as the selector for which epitaph string to display.
Present the chosen epitaph text to the player; parameters: small flag/word (from [BP-04h]), epitaph string pointer, and item/pid context.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles player interaction with a gravestone item: a short 'U8P_GRAVE_NS::look' description is shown when examined, and a more detailed epitaph reader runs when the item is used.
